sql_injection.description: MASVS-CODE-4: Untrusted input must not be concatenated into SQL; use bound parameters / Room / safe builders.

webview_xss.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-2: WebViews must not expose sensitive data or bridges to untrusted origins without hardening.

insecure_webview.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-2: Dangerous WebSettings (file/universal access, mixed content) violate secure WebView use.

hardcoded_secrets.description: MASVS-STORAGE-2: Secrets must not be embedded in source; use Keystore, remote attestation, or server-held keys.

insecure_storage.description: MASVS-STORAGE-1: Sensitive data at rest requires platform-backed encryption and least-privilege file modes.

insecure_file_permissions.description: MASVS-STORAGE-1: World-readable/writable files or legacy modes break confidentiality of stored data.

insecure_random_number_generation.description: MASVS-CRYPTO-1: Security-sensitive randomness must use SecureRandom (or platform crypto), not java.util.Random.

biometric_bypass.description: MASVS-AUTH-2: Local authentication flows must use BiometricPrompt/crypto objects correctly without trivial bypass.

intent_spoofing.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-1: IPC surfaces must enforce permissions, caller checks, and explicit intents where required.

exported_components.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-1: Exported components need explicit protection (signature permissions, exported=false, intent validation).

webview_deeplink.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-2: Deep links that drive WebView loads require URL allow-lists and safe WebView configuration.

deeplink_hijack.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-1: Intent filters and autoVerify/host scoping must prevent link hijack and unsafe VIEW handling.

path_traversal.description: MASVS-CODE-4: File paths from untrusted input must be canonicalized and confined to intended roots.

jetpack_compose_security.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-3: UI layers must avoid leaking sensitive pixels (FLAG_SECURE) and debug-only behaviour in release.

graphql_injection.description: MASVS-CODE-4: GraphQL documents built from untrusted strings enable injection and schema abuse.

pending_intent_hijacking.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-1: PendingIntents must be immutable unless a tightly scoped mutable intent is unavoidable and safe.

fragment_injection.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-1: PreferenceActivity / fragment plumbing must validate fragment class names against an allow-list.

zip_slip.description: MASVS-CODE-4: Archive extraction must validate each ZipEntry path against a trusted base directory (ZipSlip).

deeplink_logic_bypass.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-1: Deep-link parameters must not drive auth, redirects, or WebView loads without server-side/session checks.

unsafe_reflection.description: MASVS-CODE-4: Reflection and class loading from untrusted strings are high-risk dynamic code paths.

webview_file_access.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-2: File URL access from WebView expands XSS/local file read impact; disable unless strictly required.

insecure_deserialization.description: MASVS-CODE-4: Deserializing attacker-controlled object graphs enables gadget chains and RCE.

strandhogg.description: MASVS-PLATFORM-3: Task affinity and launchMode combinations can enable task hijacking / overlay attacks.

hardcoded_secrets_xml.description: MASVS-STORAGE-2: Resource XML must not ship recoverable secrets; treat APK contents as public.

library_vulnerability.description: MASVS-CODE-2: Third-party code must not undermine app integrity (droppers, hidden JNI, surveillance APIs).

universal_logic_flaw.description: MASVS-CODE-4: Business and IPC logic must validate trust boundaries, serialization, and attacker-controlled branches.
